content: We can model Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems after the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction mechanism observed in human memory to build a next-generation active learning memory agent where the hippocampus-like vector retrieval module dynamically prioritizes which unlabeled external data to fetch for the prefrontal cortex-like generator to fine-tune on, turning passive RAG retrieval into active open-world knowledge growth. This biologically grounded architecture outperforms static 2024 SOTA active learning memory agents by aligning retrieval sampling with the human brain's native efficient active learning strategy.
source_topics: memory as active learning agent SOTA 2025
